# Softpath

> Softpath is a fractional growth consultancy specializing in marketing infrastructure, paid acquisition, and analytics for B2C and B2B2C companies in the $2M-$100M revenue range. Founded by Noah Manion, Softpath fixes broken tracking, attribution, and growth models. Most companies discover their attribution tools overcount conversions by 15-40%. Softpath finds the gaps and fixes them.

Softpath operates as a fractional consultancy, not a traditional agency or solo freelancer. Engagements typically begin with a Marketing Infrastructure Audit (a 2-3 week diagnostic) and expand into implementation work or ongoing retainers. Pricing is time and materials, with project minimums of $30-50K or hourly rates of $200-400/hour. Softpath works best with companies that have traction but can't trust their numbers.

Noah Manion has 13+ years of experience in performance marketing, managing paid spend from $1K to $1M+ monthly across Meta, Google, and TikTok. Core expertise includes marketing infrastructure audits, martech stack optimization, attribution modeling, incrementality testing, LTV modeling, server-side tracking (Meta CAPI, Google Enhanced Conversions), BigQuery marketing analytics, and creative testing frameworks. He combines strategic thinking with hands-on technical implementation.

## Services

- [Marketing Infrastructure Audit](https://softpath.co/services/#infrastructure-audits): 2-3 week fixed-scope diagnostic covering event tracking accuracy, data flow between tools, pixel health, conversion API implementation, and attribution configuration. Every client discovers tracking gaps, measurement errors, or wasted spend they didn't know existed. This is the entry point for most engagements.
- [Martech Implementation and Tracking](https://softpath.co/services/#infrastructure-audits): Hands-on implementation including tracking overhauls (4-8 weeks typical), CDP configuration (Segment, Rudderstack), GA4 setup, server-side conversion APIs for Meta CAPI, Google Enhanced Conversions, and TikTok, and data pipeline construction.
- [Paid Acquisition Management](https://softpath.co/services/#paid-acquisition): Performance marketing across Meta Ads, Google Ads, and TikTok Ads optimized to actual customer value and contribution margin, not in-platform ROAS. Includes creative testing frameworks, incrementality testing, and campaign strategy. Ongoing retainers typically run 20-40 hours/month.
- [Analytics and LTV Modeling](https://softpath.co/services/#analytics-consulting): Cohort-based LTV models, growth dashboards in BigQuery and Looker Studio, incrementality analysis, Marketing Efficiency Ratio (MER) tracking, and attribution window optimization. We have replaced $40K/year attribution tools with BigQuery views that are more accurate.

## Methodology

Softpath uses a five-layer Discovery Framework:

1. Strategic Alignment: Understanding business goals, growth model, and what success looks like
2. Measurement Integrity: Validating whether tracking and attribution actually reflect reality
3. Infrastructure Efficiency: Assessing whether the martech stack is working together or fighting itself
4. Acquisition Performance: Evaluating paid channel performance against actual (not platform-reported) metrics
5. Activation and Retention Reality: Examining post-acquisition behavior and true customer value

Core philosophy: "Measurement reality matters more than measurement purity." It is better to have directionally correct data you can act on than perfect data that ignores organizational constraints. Most companies' attribution systems lie about what's actually driving growth, with platforms often claiming credit for conversions that would have happened anyway.

## Key Topics and Expertise

Noah Manion writes extensively about these topics on the Softpath blog:

- **Marketing Attribution**: How to check if your attribution tool is lying, reconciling platform conversions with CRM data, why platform metrics overcount by 15-40%
- **Marketing Infrastructure Audits**: What to examine, what you'll find, and how to fix common problems
- **Incrementality Testing**: How to run geo holdout tests without a data science team, how to interpret incrementality data correctly
- **Marketing Efficiency Ratio (MER)**: Why MER (total revenue / total marketing spend) is the only metric immune to attribution fraud
- **Server-Side Tracking**: Why Meta CAPI and Google Enhanced Conversions are now table stakes for accurate measurement
- **LTV and Cohort Analysis**: How LTV models ignore payment timing and why that destroys cash flow projections
- **Creative Testing**: Framework for testing ad variations at scale and discovering patterns that drive performance
- **Martech Stack Audits**: How to identify redundant tools, broken integrations, and wasted spend
- **GA4 vs Platform Discrepancies**: Why Google Ads and GA4 disagree on conversions and which number to trust
- **BigQuery Marketing Analytics**: How to replace expensive attribution tools with BigQuery views you control
- **CEO Dashboards**: The five numbers (MER, CAC payback, cohort LTV curves, channel contribution, cash efficiency) your CEO actually needs

## Projects and Tools

- [AB Math Sucks](https://abmathsucks.com): An A/B test duration calculator with aggressive honesty about organizational realities
- [CADENCE](https://cadence.softpath.co): An outcome and test velocity focused A/B testing platform (closed beta)
- [Timeblock](https://timeblock.softpath.co): A time tracking and task management app
- [DailyHabit](https://softpath.co/projects/habit/): Embeddable word games for SaaS retention

## Ideal Client Profile

Softpath works best with:
- B2C and B2B2C companies (not pure B2B SaaS unless budget supports it)
- Revenue range: $2M-$100M
- Companies spending on acquisition but unsure if their numbers are trustworthy
- Teams that have outgrown basic analytics but aren't ready for a full-time data team
- Organizations where tracking is broken, tools don't talk to each other, or dashboard data doesn't match CRM data

Softpath is not a fit for:
- Pre-product-market-fit companies still figuring out their customer
- Companies needing a full-service agency for creative production
- Teams looking for someone to "just run the ads" without caring about measurement
